title: Logic Gates
summary: AND, OR, NOT, NAND, NOR, XOR — the atomic building blocks of digital logic.

sections.heading: What Is a Logic Gate?
sections.body: A logic gate is a physical implementation of a Boolean function. CMOS transistors form pull-up and pull-down networks that drive output HIGH (VDD) or LOW (GND) based on input voltages. In simulation and RTL, we abstract gates as ideal 0/1 elements.
bullets: Inputs and outputs are discrete logic levels
Propagation delay: output changes after inputs settle
Fan-out: how many inputs one output can drive

sections.heading: Primitive Gates
sections.body: The six common gates each implement one Boolean operation. Truth tables completely specify single-output combinational gate behavior.
bullets: AND: output 1 only if all inputs are 1
OR: output 1 if any input is 1
NOT (inverter): output is complement of input
XOR: output 1 if odd number of 1s (for 2 inputs: inputs differ)
NAND: complement of AND — universal gate
NOR: complement of OR — also universal

sections.heading: Universal Gates
sections.body: NAND and NOR are universal: any Boolean function can be built using only NAND gates (or only NOR gates). This is why NAND flash and standard-cell libraries often prefer NAND-based implementations.

callout.text: NOT can be built as NAND(A,A) or NOR(A,A). AND from NANDs: NAND(NAND(A,B), NAND(A,B)).

sections.heading: Gates in Verilog
sections.body: Structural Verilog instantiates primitive gates directly. Behavioral RTL uses assign statements and always_comb blocks. Synthesis maps both to standard-cell gates.
bullets: xor, nand, nor, not, buf primitives exist in Verilog

sections.heading: Gate Delay and Glitches
sections.body: Real gates have non-zero delay (tpd). When inputs change, intermediate values may briefly appear at the output (hazards/glitches) before settling. Hazard-free design matters in asynchronous inputs and critical control paths.

quiz.question: A 2-input NAND gate outputs 0 when:
quiz.explanation: NAND = NOT(AND). Output is 0 only when both inputs are 1.

quiz.question: Which gates are universal?
quiz.explanation: NAND and NOR alone can implement any Boolean function.

quiz.question: How many rows in a 3-input gate truth table?
quiz.explanation: 2³ = 8 input combinations for three binary inputs.

quiz.question: Fan-out refers to:
options: Number of inputs one output can drive
quiz.explanation: Fan-out is the load capacity of an output — how many gate inputs it can drive reliably.

flashcards.front: Truth table rows for n inputs?
2-input OR symbol output when A=0, B=1?

flashcards.back: NOT(XOR) — output 1 when inputs are equal
Propagation delay — time from input change to stable output.
